answer: This module provides runtime support for type hints.
The Python runtime does not enforce function and variable type annotations.

The use of a type alias declares two types to be equivalent to one another, making the static type checker treat them as exactly equivalent in all cases. In contrast, NewType declares one type to be a subtype of another, making the static type checker treat the new type as a subclass of the original type, which means a value of the original type cannot be used in places where a value of the new type is expected.
A special kind of type is Any. A static type checker will treat every type as being compatible with Any and Any as being compatible with every type.
